title: rootless podman containers Error: unable to obtain cgroup stats:
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**
podman stats  not working :
Error: unable to obtain cgroup stats: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-06e04ef2d62db292f5981bcb9edbfab7947c22f6c0da8234a9e076d130af858d/pids.current: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-06e04ef2d62db292f5981bcb9edbfab7947c22f6c0da8234a9e076d130af858d/pids.current: no such file or directory
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.Run Podman under rootless user with cgroupv2

2. install crun

3.Change OCI runtime to crun

**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:      2.0.6
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.14
Built:        Tue Sep  1 15:27:34 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

comments.body: Actually, let's hand this one to @ParkerVR
Hi @haykhovsepyan , I followed your reproducer but did not encounter the error. I will work to improve the message anyways, but can you provide a specific reproducer?

@ParkerVR  tested on distribution CentOS Linux release 8.2.2004 (Core),
`kernel 4.18.0-193.14.2.el8_2.x86_64`

cgroup v2 , OCIRuntime crun,

Problem with "podman stats"
```
podman stats --no-stream
Error: unable to obtain cgroup stats: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-06e04ef2d62db292f5981bcb9edbfab7947c22f6c0da8234a9e076d130af858d/pids.current: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-06e04ef2d62db292f5981bcb9edbfab7947c22f6c0da8234a9e076d130af858d/pids.current: no such file or directory


```
or

```
podman stats
Error: unable to obtain cgroup stats: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-06e04ef2d62db292f5981bcb9edbfab7947c22f6c0da8234a9e076d130af858d/memory.current: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-06e04ef2d62db292f5981bcb9edbfab7947c22f6c0da8234a9e076d130af858d/memory.current: no such file or directory
```
also see cgroup mount
```
mount -l | grep cgroup
cgroup2 on /sys/fs/cgroup type cgroup2 (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel,nsdelegate)
```
```
podman --log-level=debug
Failed to add podman to systemd sandbox cgroup: dbus: authentication failed
```

@mheon  but systems runs with cgroup v2.

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.1
  cgroupVersion: v2
```

Oh, that's very interesting... I wonder if this is a cgroupfs vs systemd cgroup problem.

Can you try creating the container and running `podman stats` with `--cgroup-manager=systemd` and seeing if that changes anything?

it`s not even works on root user.

```
podman stats --cgroup-manager=systemd
Error: unable to obtain cgroup stats: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-c00cf2c7c635e0c2eb4ee2d4614a621c132194712f0a021c1d25ea753ec7f650.scope/memory.current: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-c00cf2c7c635e0c2eb4ee2d4614a621c132194712f0a021c1d25ea753ec7f650.scope/memory.current: no such file or directory
```
like with systemd PATH cahnged to .scope.

also maybe this can help 
```
ls -l /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-c00cf2c7c635e0c2eb4ee2d4614a621c132194712f0a021c1d25ea753ec7f650/
total 0
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.controllers
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.events
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.freeze
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.max.depth
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.max.descendants
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.procs
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.stat
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.subtree_control
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.threads
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cgroup.type
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cpu.pressure
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 cpu.stat
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 io.pressure
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 memory.pressure
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 pids.current
-r--r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 pids.events
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Sep  9 08:40 pids.max
```

cgroup-manager changed to "systemd".

```
"ExitCommand": [
            "/usr/bin/podman",
            "--root",
            "/home/wmcontainers/.local/share/containers/storage",
            "--runroot",
            "/run/user/1000/containers",
            "--log-level",
            "error",
            "--cgroup-manager",
            "systemd",
            "--tmpdir",
            "/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp",
            "--runtime",
            "/usr/bin/crun",
            "--storage-driver",
            "overlay",
            "--storage-opt",
            "overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs",
            "--events-backend",
            "file",
            "container",
            "cleanup",
            "43cc57a41f814bf3c5b86335fc799f009b86ad988132ab5cd0ea42ecb5355a70"
```
started work under root. But under rootless unable to start containers: 
`Error: unable to start container "d5fde2d8343dc9ad69632dbf13d041532c5f76fd2544e3899e54cc834a43ad6f": sd-bus call: Permission denied: OCI runtime permission denied error`

Systemd version 
```
systemctl --version
systemd 239
```

On fedora 32 it works with systemd version 243.

Can problem cause of  old systemd version that come on Centos 8?  However systemd version 239 is the latest for Centos.

Older systemd does not handle cgroupV2 at all, we are seeing this issue with RHEL7/Centos7 systemd based containers.  I was not aware of issues with Centos8.  We had some discussions with the systemd maintainers, I guess they do some hacking stuff in systemd-nspawn to attempt to figure out the version of systemd within the container and then setup the environment in cgroup V1 emulation mode.  Not sure if we want to do this in Podman.

Are you confirm that systemd version 239 on latest centos 8 OS is not compatibly  with rootless containers cgroupv2 enabled.?
And  what is systemd compatibility version  with rootless podman (cgroupv2 enabled)?

ubi8-init runs fine on cgroups V2 systems.

sh-4.4# rpm -q systemd
systemd-239-31.el8_2.2.x86_64

```
# podman run -ti ubi8-init
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/ubi8-init...
```

It works under root user. But under rootless:

```
[user@dev188 ~]$ podman run -ti ubi8-init
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8-init...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c4d668e229cd done  
Copying blob 8a58a033071f done  
Copying blob ec1681b6a383 done  
Copying config 33c0991371 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: sd-bus call: Permission denied: OCI runtime permission denied error
```
How did you log into that user - using `sudo` or `su`? Those two both don't create a full user session, and as such the user's systemd instance won't be available.

I use login session just from ssh  user, not su - user. 
just for note:

```
[user@dev188 1000]$ echo $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR && echo $DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
/run/user/1000
unix:path=/run/user/1000/bus
```
journalctl 

`conmon d5fde2d8343dc9ad6963 <ndebug>: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied`

Maybe problem related with user systemd instance? I found the following problem also.

```
[root@dev188 ~]# journalctl -u user@1000
Failed to connect to API bus: Connection refused
```
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1764282 
It's not been fixed by Redhat yet. The fix is scheduled for 8.4.  

but I'm not sure if this is the problem
@giuseppe PTAL
@giuseppe can you give some feedback?
does it work if you run `systemd-run --scope --user echo it works` ?  Do you get any error?

```
[user@dev188 ~]$ systemd-run --scope --user echo it works
Running scope as unit: run-ra69cc7fe36ba4d70bcecf4c2c22e8e81.scope
it works
```
yes

Hi,
Any update on this situation?

can you try the command: `unshare -r systemd-run --scope --user echo it works` ?

If it doesn't work then it means the systemd version is too old and doesn't support using dbus from a user namespace (it requires https://github.com/systemd/systemd/pull/11785)

From rootless user
```
$ unshare -r systemd-run --scope --user echo it works
Failed to start transient scope unit: Access denied
```
Under root

```
# unshare -r systemd-run --scope --user echo it works
Running scope as unit: run-rf82a0490227747e7b9d9af186ee1d529.scope
it works
```
`systemd 239`

systemd  latest version for Centos 8 (cgroups v2) 

Error:

`sd-bus call: Permission denied: OCI runtime permission denied error`

thanks for confirming it.  So that is the issue, dbus doesn't work from a user namespace.  I am going to close the issue as there is nothing we can do from Podman and it is already fixed upstream in systemd.  Next centos version will have the fix as well.

@giuseppe  thanks for information , will wait centos next version release. 
You can close the issue.
